description: Unused Security Group Open to the Internet
rationale: This security group has an ingress rule open to the whole internet (0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0) but is not attached to any resource. Because nothing is using it, there is currently no internet-reachable resource behind this rule, so the real-world exposure is low - but the permissive, unused group is still worth cleaning up: it could later be attached to a resource and silently expose it, and it adds noise that hides genuinely risky groups. The higher-severity 'Security Group Opens ...' findings still report the permissive rule itself; this low-severity finding adds the context that this particular group is not in use.
remediation: Delete the unused security group, or if it is kept intentionally, tighten its ingress rules to the minimum source ranges needed so it is not internet-open if it is later attached to a resource.
